# A Note on Tone

Good writing has rhythm — and rhythm needs variety. A long sentence can wander gracefully through a thought, gathering clauses as it goes, before it finally arrives at its point. Then a short one lands. The contrast is the music — and an em dash, used now and then, adds a sharp turn. But only now and then — overuse flattens the effect entirely. A page of dashes reads as breathless — and breathless prose tires the reader quickly. Restraint is the real craft here.
